article_title: Exa Raises $250 Million Series C at $2.2 Billion Valuation to Build Search Infrastructure for AI Agents

content_preview.title: Exa Raises $250 Million Series C at $2.2 Billion Valuation to Build Search Infrastructure for AI Agents
content_preview.summary: Andreessen Horowitz leads a $250M Series C in Exa, valuing the AI-native search startup at $2.2B as it serves over 5,000 companies including Cursor, Cognition, and HubSpot.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Exa, the San Francisco-based startup building a search engine designed for artificial intelligence rather than human users, raised $250 million in a Series C round led by Andreessen Horowitz, the company announced on May 20, 2026. The funding values Exa at $2.2 billion, according to [SiliconAngle](https://siliconangle.com/2026/05/20/exa-labs-raises-250m-2-2b-valuation-ai-search-tools/), and comes as the broader market for AI-native infrastructure draws intensifying venture capital i...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article covering a significant funding event. Clear 'What We Know / What We Don't Know' format works well. Technical details (vector database, exa-d, embedding models) add depth without becoming jargon-heavy. The 'What We Don't Know' section is a notable editorial strength that demonstrates appropriate epistemic humility.
editor_notes.source_verification: All three sources fetched with HTTP 200. Snapshots read: source-0.html.gz (SiliconAngle), source-1.html.gz (PYMNTS), source-2.html.gz (TechStartups). Key facts verified: $250M raise (all sources), $2.2B valuation (all sources), a16z as lead investor (SiliconAngle — written 'Andersen Horowitz', clearly a typo; article correctly renders 'Andreessen Horowitz'), 5,000 companies (PYMNTS), 400,000 developers (SiliconAngle), Cursor/Cognition/HubSpot/OpenRouter/Monday.com clients (PYMNTS), $85M Series B with Nvidia and Y Combinator (SiliconAngle), Exa Instant <180ms (SiliconAngle), vector database one-tenth of a second (SiliconAngle), exa-d platform parallelizes across GPUs (SiliconAngle), custom embedding models on Nvidia cluster (SiliconAngle), Bryk quotes (PYMNTS and SiliconAngle), TechStartups 'cost of high-precision, AI-native retrieval' quote (exact match). No unverified claims found. All direct quotes verified as verbatim or accurately paraphrased from the cited source.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quantitative claims (funding amount, valuation, customer count, developer count, latency spec, prior round size) trace to cited sources. The lead investor attribution (a16z/Andreessen Horowitz) is supported by SiliconAngle's text ('Andersen Horowitz' — an evident typographic error for the well-known firm). TechStartups lists the lead investor as 'Undisclosed', but does not contradict the a16z attribution. No fabricated statistics or hallucinated quotes detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No editorializing. Bryk's forward-looking statements are clearly attributed as his own framing, not presented as fact. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately signals limitations of available information.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Exa coverage found in the archive. Distinct from any existing article. The funding round was announced May 20, 2026 — this is timely, original coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean, well-sourced News article. All facts verified against snapshots, no fabrications or misattributions detected, strong structure with appropriate epistemic caveats. Ready for publication.
